benchmark_type: GPU execution timing — calibration measurement artifact

kernel_identity.digest_match: EXACT — GPU kernel produces identical digests to C99 reference

gpu_small_v2.calibration_role: T2 data point for gpu-small-v2 (non-floor, 12 GB not 8 GB)

gpu_medium_v2.calibration_role: NEGATIVE CONTROL for gpu-medium-v2 (T2 ceiling, 12 GB)

gpu_large_v2.failure_mode: OOM — need 12.96 GB, only 11.59 GB free
gpu_large_v2.calibration_role: Confirms VRAM wall: 12 GB device cannot fit 12.96 GB working set

compute_competitiveness.positive_device: RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (16 GB, T3 floor)
compute_competitiveness.slowdown_explanation: Proportional to hardware specs: bandwidth ratio 504/672=0.75, SM ratio 56/66=0.85. No class-level compute gap.

vram_discrimination.gpu_small_v2_4gb: FITS both devices — no VRAM discrimination at this working set
vram_discrimination.gpu_medium_v2_8gb: FITS both devices — 8.64 GB < 12 GB T2 ceiling. Discrimination occurs at T1 boundary (6 GB would OOM)
vram_discrimination.gpu_large_v2_13gb: OOM on 12 GB device — VRAM wall confirmed. Binary discrimination between T3 (fits) and T2 (does not fit)

evidence_status: BENCHMARK DATA — standalone GPU timing without challenge protocol. Protocol timing will be higher by network RTT + serialization. compute_slowdown computed from GPU-only medians as per calibration plan.
